meta.title: The correlation between morphological and phylogenetic classification of myxobacteria
meta.journalTitle: International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology

abstractHtml: In order to determine whether morphological criteria are suitable to affiliate myxobacterial strains to species, a phylogenetic analysis of 16S rDNAs was performed on 54 myxobacterial strains that represented morphologically 21 species of the genera Angiococcus, Archangium, Chondromyces, Cystobacter, Melittangium, Myxococcus, Polyangium and Stigmatella, five invalid species and three unclassified isolates. The analysis included 12 previously published sequences. The branching pattern confirmed the deep trifurcation of the order Myxococcales. One lineage is defined by the genera Cystobacter, Angiococcus, Archangium, Melittangium, Myxococcus and Stigmatella. The study confirms the genus status of 'Corallococcus', previously 'Chondrococcus', within the family Myxococcaceae. The second lineage contains the genus Chondromyces and the species Polyangium ('Sorangium') cellulosum, while the third lineage is comprised of Nannocystis and a strain identified as Polyangium vitellinum. With the exception of a small number of strains that did not cluster phylogenetically with members of the genus to which they were assigned by morphological criteria ('Polyangium thaxteri' Pl t3, Polyangium cellulosum ATCC 25531(T), Melittangium lichenicola ATCC 25947(T) and Angiococcus disciformis An d1), the phenotypic classification should provide a sound basis for the description of neotype species in those cases where original strain material is not available or is listed as reference material.
